reasoning: The cessation of Russian gas transit through Ukraine on January 1, 2025 left TurkStream and LNG as the only active vectors for Russian natural gas into the EU. According to the background information, total Russian exports to Europe fell consistently into mid-2025, reaching just 3.01 bcm in June, part of a broader 9.4% quarter-on-quarter drop. To hit the 19 bcm threshold for the entirety of H2 2025 (Q3 and Q4), imports would need to average at least 3.16 bcm/month. With the ongoing downward trend and increasing political efforts to phase out Russian fossil fuels entirely by 2027 (REPowerEU), achieving a sustained rate safely above the June levels for six consecutive months is highly improbable. The lack of Ukrainian transit structurally caps the upside for Russian pipeline gas, and LNG imports face ongoing political scrutiny, despite a slight delay in restrictions. Both structural constraints and recent volume trends strongly suggest that H2 imports will fall short of the 19 bcm target. I forecast 8%, aligning closely with the crowd's 9%.
